api/client/python/openmeter/_generated.purpose: Root of the auto-generated Python SDK for OpenMeter. Exposes OpenMeterClient (sync) as the sole public entry point; every resource group is an attribute instantiated in __init__ sharing one PipelineClient, Serializer, and Deserializer. Generated by `make gen-api` — never hand-edit except _patch.py.

patterns.name: Single shared PipelineClient with fixed policy chain
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient.__init__ builds exactly one PipelineClient with a fixed policy list (headers, user-agent, proxy, ContentDecode, retry, auth, logging) and passes it to every Operations constructor. Never create a second PipelineClient inside an Operations class.

patterns.name: Operations-as-attributes
patterns.description: Each resource group is a named attribute set in __init__, receiving (self._client, self._config, self._serialize, self._deserialize). Access via client.customers.list_customers(), never by instantiating an Operations class directly.

patterns.name: send_request {endpoint} URL formatting
patterns.description: send_request deepcopies the request and resolves the {endpoint} placeholder via self._client.format_url before dispatch; a raw URL passed without this resolution produces a malformed request.

patterns.name: _patch.py customization escape hatch
patterns.description: _patch.py is the ONLY file preserved across regenerations. Add public symbols to its __all__ and implement patch_sdk() for post-generation monkey-patching; __init__.py merges _patch_all into __all__ and calls patch_sdk() automatically. patch_sdk() must stay present and callable.
patterns.example: from ._patch import __all__ as _patch_all; __all__.extend([p for p in _patch_all if p not in __all__])

patterns.name: Context-manager protocol on client
patterns.description: OpenMeterClient implements __enter__/__exit__ delegating to self._client; always use a with-statement or call close() to release the underlying HTTP connection pool, after draining any streaming responses.

patterns.name: Tagged-union type aliases in _types.py
patterns.description: _types.py defines discriminated-union aliases (App, Entitlement, RateCard, NotificationRule, SubscriptionEditOperation, ...) under a TYPE_CHECKING guard to avoid circular imports; these are the canonical param/return annotations across Operations and must not be imported at runtime outside annotations.

key_file_guides.role: Sync entry point — constructs PipelineClient, policy chain, Serializer/Deserializer, and all Operations attributes; defines send_request, close, __enter__/__exit__.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated — any hand-edit is overwritten by make gen-api. Adding a resource group requires regeneration, not editing here.

key_file_guides.role: OpenMeterClientConfiguration: stores endpoint, polling_interval, and all corehttp policy instances; injected into every Operations constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: authentication_policy defaults to None — callers must pass one via kwargs or requests go out unauthenticated.

key_file_guides.role: Only file safe to edit; survives regeneration. patch_sdk() runs after __init__.py imports complete.
key_file_guides.watch_for: patch_sdk() must remain present and callable — removing it breaks the __init__.py import. Put retry/auth via a custom policy kwarg, not business logic here.

key_file_guides.role: Defines tagged-union Python type aliases (App, Entitlement, RateCard, NotificationRule, etc.) used as param/return annotations across Operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated; uses TYPE_CHECKING guard — do not import these at runtime outside type annotations.

key_file_guides.role: Pins the SDK version string used by sdk_moniker in OpenMeterClientConfiguration's user-agent.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Value is 0.0.0 in source; the real version is injected at release build time. Do not hardcode a real version here.

key_file_guides.role: Public surface: re-exports OpenMeterClient and VERSION; merges _patch_all into __all__ and calls patch_sdk().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Generated — do not add imports here; use _patch.py for any custom exports.

key_file_guides.role: Child: async (asyncio) variant — AsyncPipelineClient + async Operations; execution/deserialization only, all request-building delegated to the sync operations layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Same generated-file rules apply; await response.read() before raising on a streaming error to avoid leaking the connection.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing _client.py, __init__.py, _version.py, or operations/_operations.py — all overwritten by make gen-api.
Instantiating Operations classes directly instead of accessing them through client attributes — bypasses the shared serializer/deserializer and config.
Passing a raw URL to send_request without resolving the {endpoint} placeholder via format_url — produces malformed URLs.
Adding retry or authentication logic inside _patch.py instead of injecting a custom policy via the policies kwarg to OpenMeterClient.
Calling client.close() without first draining streaming responses — leaks HTTP connections in the PipelineClient pool.

decisions.decision: All request-building lives in child operations/_operations.py build_*_request helpers; _client.py only wires the pipeline.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the client thin and regenerable; schema-specific serialization is isolated to child packages so regeneration scope stays contained.

decisions.decision: OpenMeterClientConfiguration is a separate injectable class rather than raw kwargs.
decisions.rationale: Lets callers share or replace a config object (e.g. swap authentication_policy) without reconstructing the client.

decisions.decision: _patch.py is the sole file preserved across regenerations as a customization boundary.
decisions.rationale: Prevents hand-edits from being silently lost on the next gen-api run while still allowing SDK-level monkey-patching via patch_sdk().

code_examples.scenario: Instantiate the sync client with a bearer-token auth policy and call a resource group method
code_examples.code:
from corehttp.runtime import policies
from openmeter._generated import OpenMeterClient

auth = policies.BearerTokenCredentialPolicy(credential, "scope")
client = OpenMeterClient(
    endpoint="https://api.openmeter.cloud",
    authentication_policy=auth,
)
with client:
    customers = client.customers.list_customers()
